title: Highway 10 - Saudipediasaudipedia.com › highway-10
description: Highway 10 is a major roadway in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, stretching from the south to the east. It serves as a key artery in the Saudi land transportation network.
content:
| Feature | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| Official Length | 240 km |
| Starting Endpoint | Harad Center in al-Ahsa Governorate |
| Finishing Endpoint | al-Batha Border Crossing with the United Arab Emirates |
| Source of Measurement | Guinness World Records |
| Date of Record Entry | September 3, 2020 |

* **Record Status**: Longest straight road in the world without curves.
* **Segment Location**: Running from Harad Center in al-Ahsa Governorate to the Saudi border with the United Arab Emirates at al-Batha Border Crossing.
* **Road Description**: Single, dual, and express roads featuring two lanes separated by protective elements (steel crash barriers or concrete protective walls).
* **Safety Features**: Asphalted shoulders with improved side slopes, painted road edges and medians, reflective road markers ("cat's eyes"), protective barriers, kilometer markers, and directional, warning, and guidance signs.
